explanation: TRUE POSITIVE. The swap() function in Swap.sol accepts any arbitrary ERC20 token address as payment (only checking token != address(0)) with no whitelist. SafeERC20.safeTransferFrom only checks that the call does not revert and that returned data is not false — it does not verify that token balances actually changed. The buyer (attacker) controls data.token by signing the SwapData struct themselves, and can specify a malicious ERC20 that implements transferFrom to return true without moving any tokens. This allows the seller's ERC-1155 NFT to be transferred to the attacker at line 134 while the payment call at line 138 silently succeeds with zero tokens moved. All 6 gate reviews pass: attacker controls the token address, the attack is mathematically trivial (deploy a lying ERC20), the impact is direct asset loss (NFT theft with no payment), and no environmental or code-level protection prevents exploitation.
